step_by_step_thinking:
I agree with the provided solution. The key point here is the application of the empirical rule for normally distributed data, which states that approximately 99.7% of values fall within three standard deviations (\( \pm 3\sigma \)) of the mean (\( \mu \)). This directly corresponds to "99.73 variation" indicating \( \pm 3 \) SDs as described.

Therefore, my final answer remains: **C**. Three Standard Deviations. Agreeing fully with the initial assessment based on well-established statistical norms.
